en.title: Tracking inequalities within and across countries: a new approach
todo
Phasing out fossil-fuel subsidies: can it be done?
en.abstract: The remarkable economic progress the world has experienced until now has largely been powered by fossil energy. The increased levels and variety in consumption are also anthropogenic factors contributing to the record levels of global warming evident in our world today. SDG 12 calls for responsible means of production and consumption that ensure sustainable development in the long run for all people on the planet. This story focuses on the real political and geopolitical issues that have undermined progress in the removal of fossil-fuel subsidies around the world and suggests reforms to address these issues.

en.title: Vulnerability and adaptation to climate-related hazards
en.abstract: Extreme weather events, such as floods, droughts, tornadoes, and heatwaves  occur more often and are stronger due to climate change and impact more than half the world’s population. A third of those affected are also highly vulnerable and require adaptation to a changing climate.

en.title: The impact of early education on work prospects
en.abstract: Education is one of the most powerful drivers of growth and poverty reduction. Each year of schooling improves earnings by around 10%. However, learning doesn’t end at school. Around half of the skills that workers use are acquired at work.
en.inputs: Norbert Schady, Joana Silva, Alaka Holla
